C003.summary: The original problem is solved. Agent Monitor activity titles now have a structured backend-to-frontend public field, the frontend consumes that field as title authority, and reasoning text is no longer parsed for title keywords. The screenshot failure mode cannot recur through the old heuristic path.

P000.description:
Activity Monitor currently projects public row titles in the frontend by inspecting private reasoning text. This produced incorrect non-model-generated titles such as `正在组织回复` when the reasoning content merely contained the word `回复`. The long-term architecture should make monitor titles a structured public projection instead of UI keyword guessing, while preserving agent reasoning detail as the detailed text when available.

Scope includes the runtime activity projection, shared/app entity contract, frontend normalization/rendering, regression tests, and repository commit hygiene for the touched subrepos and parent gitlink.
success_criteria: Runtime activity records expose an explicit structured public title field for monitor UI.
Frontend renders activity titles from structured public fields or safe phase/status fallback, not from reasoning text keywords.
Agent reasoning content can still be shown as detail text; only heuristic title generation is removed.
Existing read/reply/tool/summary monitor behavior remains user-facing and low-level shell labels remain hidden.
Tests cover the screenshot regression: reasoning text containing `回复` must not render `正在组织回复` unless that title is explicitly provided as a public title.
Relevant frontend and runtime tests pass, and unrelated lint failures are documented if they pre-exist.
Changes are committed in affected subrepos and the parent repo records updated submodule pointers.
P000.title: Make Agent Monitor activity titles structured and non-heuristic

P001.description: The runtime currently emits `agent-activity-records` with generic `title` and private/detail `text`, while the frontend invents some public titles by reading reasoning text. P002.title: Render Agent Monitor titles from public fields, not reasoning keywords

T000.classification_reason: Cross-repo contract/runtime/frontend change with independent verification paths; split keeps schema, UI, tests, and commit hygiene explicit.
T000.problem_definition:
Agent Monitor title projection currently mixes two layers: durable runtime activity records and frontend presentation inference. The frontend reads `record.text` for reasoning records and infers public titles from keywords like `回复`, `新消息`, or `上下文`. This creates incorrect UI-owned step titles that do not correspond to a real runtime event and are hard to reason about, test, or localize.

The desired long-term shape is explicit: runtime activity projection writes public presentation fields for the monitor, while private reasoning text remains detail content. The frontend should render public title fields when present and fall back only to structured phase/status labels. It must not inspect reasoning text to invent titles.
T000.proposed_solution:
Introduce an explicit `public_title` field on `agent-activity-records` and route monitor row titles through that field. Runtime projection will populate `public_title` for known user-facing records such as message observation, shell read/reply actions, summary/bookkeeping, and generic reasoning. The app entity contract and TypeScript normalization will carry the field. `ActivityTimeline` will prefer `public_title`, use structured fallbacks for legacy records, and remove all reasoning-text keyword title inference.

The implementation should keep reasoning `text` visible as detail text because that is useful agent thinking content, but title generation must no longer depend on that text.
risks: Existing records without `public_title` must still render acceptably through structured fallbacks.
Overloading `public_title` with UI copy could become a localization issue later; this change still improves correctness by making the boundary explicit.
Backend contract updates may require schema sync in Business or generated contracts if the local system has multiple contract sources.
Parent repo commit must not accidentally include unrelated submodule or generated artifact churn.
T000.title: Structured public activity titles for Agent Monitor
T000.verification_plan:
- Run focused runtime activity projection tests.
- Run focused frontend ActivityTimeline tests.
- Run ESLint or a focused lint on touched frontend files; document unrelated repo-wide lint failures if present.
- Review diffs for contract, projection, normalization, rendering, and tests.
- Use the ledger success check to map all acceptance criteria to evidence.

T001.classification_reason: Contract and runtime projection change is narrow, reversible, and directly verified by focused projection tests.
T001.problem_definition: `agent-activity-records` lacks an explicit public row title field. Runtime emits `title` and `text`, but the title is not consistently the UI title, and the frontend compensated with keyword inference from `text`. The backend contract should provide `public_title` so the UI can render structured labels without reading private/detail content.
T001.proposed_solution: Add `public_title` to the shared entity contract and Business schema definition. Extend `_emit_record()` in runtime activity projection to accept and store `public_title`, defaulting to the emitted title when no override is supplied. Update projection tests to assert `public_title` exists for reasoning and tool records while preserving existing `text` behavior.
risks: Contract definitions may have duplicate copies; missing one would cause app/runtime drift.
New field must not replace `title` immediately because older consumers may still use it.
T001.title: Add public_title to backend activity projection

T002.problem_definition: The frontend must stop inventing monitor row titles from private reasoning text. It needs to consume the new explicit `public_title` field from Entangled activity records and keep only structured fallbacks for legacy records that lack the new field.
T002.proposed_solution: Add `public_title` to app-side contracts, entity types, normalized timeline records, and title projection. Make `ActivityTimeline` prefer `record.public_title` when non-empty. Keep existing read/reply low-level shell fallbacks for old records, keep phase/status fallback for reasoning records, and remove all reasoning-text keyword inference. Update tests to cover explicit public titles, legacy fallback, and the screenshot regression.
risks: Legacy records without `public_title` still need acceptable fallback.
Tests that intentionally covered old heuristic behavior must be updated carefully to preserve read/reply public behavior.
